original_registry_entry.issuing_organisation: National Institute for Health and Care Excellence
original_registry_entry.exact_document_title: Diabetic foot problems: prevention and management
original_registry_entry.version: NICE guideline NG19, last updated 11 October 2019 with selected 2023 recommendation amendments shown on the page
original_registry_entry.jurisdiction: England and Wales; international evidence requiring UAE adaptation
original_registry_entry.population: Children, young people, and adults with diabetes, with age-specific foot-assessment recommendations.
original_registry_entry.clinical_setting: Diabetes foot-risk assessment, active foot-problem documentation, multidisciplinary pathways, and follow-up.
original_registry_entry.applicability_note: Exact for clinician examination of both feet, neuropathy, ischaemia, ulceration, callus, infection or inflammation, deformity, gangrene, Charcot context, risk classification already assigned, and foot-review documentation. It does not auto-classify risk or generate referral or treatment.

exact_sections.heading: Recommendations 1.3.1–1.3.6 — frequency, bilateral foot examination, and clinician risk stratification
exact_sections.evidence_summary: Supports documenting age-appropriate review, bilateral skin and sensory examination, perfusion, ulceration, callus, infection or inflammation, deformity, gangrene, Charcot features, and clinician-entered risk classification without calculating it.

exact_sections.heading: Recommendations 1.3.10–1.3.13 — specialist assessment, reassessment, and information
exact_sections.evidence_summary: Supports documenting completed foot protection assessment, vascular or biomechanical review, clinician-selected reassessment, information already provided, and an entered follow-up plan.

title: Diabetic foot problems: prevention and management
publisher: National Institute for Health and Care Excellence
jurisdiction: England and Wales; international evidence requiring UAE adaptation
population: Children, young people, and adults with diabetes, with age-specific foot-assessment recommendations.
intended_setting: Diabetes foot-risk assessment, active foot-problem documentation, multidisciplinary pathways, and follow-up.
